lessons.title: Factors, multiples and primes
lessons.objective: Identify factors, multiples, prime numbers and prime factorisation.
lessons.summary: A factor divides exactly; a multiple is produced by multiplication.
lessons.practice: Find the HCF and LCM of 18 and 24.

lessons.title: Multiplying and dividing integers
lessons.objective: Apply sign rules accurately.
lessons.summary: Same signs give a positive result; different signs give a negative result.

lessons.title: Square roots and cube roots
lessons.objective: Recognise perfect squares and perfect cubes.
lessons.summary: Square root reverses squaring; cube root reverses cubing.

lessons.title: Indices
lessons.objective: Use index notation and simple index laws.
lessons.summary: When multiplying same bases, add powers.

lessons.title: Constructing expressions
lessons.objective: Translate words into algebraic expressions.
lessons.summary: Use letters for unknown quantities and preserve operation order.
lessons.example: Three more than twice x is 2x + 3.
lessons.practice: Write an expression for five less than 4n.

lessons.title: Formulae
lessons.objective: Substitute values and rearrange simple formulae.
lessons.summary: Replace variables carefully, using brackets for negative values.
lessons.example: If A = lw, l = 8 and w = 3, A = 24.
lessons.practice: Find v when v = u + at, u=4, a=3, t=5.

lessons.title: Expanding brackets
lessons.objective: Use the distributive law.
lessons.summary: Multiply every term inside the bracket.

lessons.title: Factorising
lessons.objective: Take out the highest common factor.
lessons.summary: Factorising reverses expansion.

lessons.title: Solving equations
lessons.objective: Use inverse operations and keep both sides balanced.
lessons.summary: Whatever is done to one side must be done to the other.

lessons.title: Inequalities
lessons.objective: Represent and solve simple inequalities.
lessons.summary: The solution is a range of values.

lessons.title: Multiplying and dividing by 0.1 and 0.01
lessons.objective: Understand decimal scaling.
lessons.summary: Dividing by 0.1 is the same as multiplying by 10.

lessons.title: Rounding
lessons.objective: Round to decimal places and significant figures.
lessons.summary: Inspect the next digit: 5 or more rounds up.

lessons.title: Estimation
lessons.objective: Estimate calculations using sensible rounding.
lessons.summary: Round inputs first, then calculate.

lessons.title: Ordering decimals
lessons.objective: Compare place values from left to right.
lessons.summary: Add trailing zeros when useful.

lessons.title: Multiplying decimals
lessons.objective: Multiply as whole numbers, then place the decimal point.
lessons.summary: Count total decimal places in both factors.

lessons.title: Dividing decimals
lessons.objective: Scale both numbers to make the divisor whole.
lessons.summary: Multiplying dividend and divisor by the same power of 10 preserves the quotient.

lessons.title: Efficient calculations
lessons.objective: Use place value and known facts.
lessons.summary: Rearrange calculations when it makes them simpler.

lessons.title: Parallel lines
lessons.objective: Use corresponding, alternate and co-interior angle facts.
lessons.summary: Corresponding and alternate angles are equal; co-interior sum to 180°.
lessons.example: If an alternate angle is 68°, the matching angle is 68°.
lessons.practice: Find a co-interior angle paired with 112°.

lessons.title: Exterior angles
lessons.objective: Use triangle exterior-angle relationships.
lessons.summary: An exterior angle equals the sum of the two opposite interior angles.
lessons.example: If opposite angles are 45° and 60°, exterior angle = 105°.
lessons.practice: Find the exterior angle when opposite interior angles are 38° and 77°.

lessons.title: Constructions
lessons.objective: Use ruler and compasses accurately.
lessons.summary: Construct perpendicular bisectors and angle bisectors without measuring.
lessons.example: A perpendicular bisector is equidistant from both endpoints.
lessons.practice: Describe how to construct a 60° angle.

lessons.title: Data collection
lessons.objective: Choose suitable primary and secondary data methods.
lessons.summary: Match the method to the question and avoid bias.
lessons.example: A survey can collect opinions; a sensor can collect continuous measurements.
lessons.practice: Design a question to measure travel time to school.

lessons.title: Sampling
lessons.objective: Understand population, sample and representative sampling.
lessons.summary: A representative sample reflects the population.
lessons.example: A stratified sample uses proportional groups.
lessons.practice: Explain why surveying only sports-club members may be biased.

lessons.title: Recurring decimals
lessons.objective: Recognise terminating and recurring decimals.
lessons.summary: A fraction terminates only when its simplified denominator has prime factors 2 and/or 5.

lessons.title: Ordering fractions
lessons.objective: Use common denominators or decimal equivalents.
lessons.summary: Compare fractions using a shared representation.

lessons.title: Mixed numbers
lessons.objective: Convert between mixed and improper fractions.
lessons.summary: Multiply the whole number by the denominator, then add the numerator.

lessons.title: Fraction operations
lessons.objective: Add, subtract, multiply and divide fractions.
lessons.summary: For division, multiply by the reciprocal.

lessons.title: Quadrilaterals and polygons
lessons.objective: Classify polygons using properties.
lessons.summary: Use sides, angles, parallel lines and symmetry.
lessons.example: A parallelogram has opposite sides parallel and equal.
lessons.practice: Compare a rhombus and a kite.

lessons.title: Circumference
lessons.objective: Use C = πd or C = 2πr.
lessons.summary: Diameter is twice the radius.
lessons.practice: Find the circumference of a circle with diameter 12 cm.

lessons.title: 3D shapes
lessons.objective: Identify faces, edges, vertices and nets.
lessons.summary: Visualise solids from different views.
lessons.example: A cube has 6 faces, 12 edges and 8 vertices.
lessons.practice: Sketch a valid net for a triangular prism.

lessons.title: Generating sequences
lessons.objective: Continue and generate sequences from rules.
lessons.summary: Apply the same rule consistently.

lessons.title: Rules
lessons.objective: Describe term-to-term and position-to-term rules.
lessons.summary: Position-to-term rules give any term directly.

lessons.title: nth term
lessons.objective: Use the nth term to find terms and solve inverse questions.
lessons.summary: Substitute the term number into the expression.

lessons.title: Functions
lessons.objective: Use function machines and mappings.
lessons.summary: A function maps each input to exactly one output.
lessons.practice: Find the input that gives 19 under x → 4x-1.

lessons.title: Percentage increase/decrease
lessons.objective: Calculate percentage change.
lessons.summary: Find the change, divide by original, multiply by 100%.
lessons.practice: Find the percentage decrease from 250 to 210.

lessons.title: Using a multiplier
lessons.objective: Use decimal multipliers efficiently.
lessons.summary: Increase by p%: multiply by 1+p/100; decrease by p%: multiply by 1-p/100.

lessons.title: Functions
lessons.objective: Represent functions using tables and graphs.
lessons.summary: Each ordered pair (x,y) becomes a plotted point.

lessons.title: Plotting
lessons.objective: Plot coordinates accurately in four quadrants.
lessons.summary: Move along x first, then y.

lessons.title: Gradient
lessons.objective: Interpret gradient as rise/run.
lessons.summary: Gradient measures steepness and direction.

lessons.title: Interpreting graphs
lessons.objective: Read meaning from real-life graphs.
lessons.summary: Check axes, units and scale before interpreting.
lessons.example: A horizontal distance-time segment means stationary.
lessons.practice: Explain a steep section on a temperature-time graph.

lessons.title: Simplifying ratios
lessons.objective: Divide all parts by their highest common factor.
lessons.summary: Ratios must be simplified consistently.

lessons.title: Sharing in a ratio
lessons.objective: Find total parts, then value of one part.
lessons.summary: Multiply one-part value by each ratio number.
lessons.example: Share 84 in 3:4: one part=12, shares 36 and 48.

lessons.title: Direct proportion
lessons.objective: Use equivalent ratios and unit rates.
lessons.summary: If one quantity doubles, the directly proportional quantity doubles.
lessons.example: 5 books cost £20, so 1 costs £4 and 8 cost £32.
lessons.practice: 6 kg costs £15; find the cost of 14 kg.

lessons.title: Calculating probabilities
lessons.objective: Use favourable outcomes over total outcomes.
lessons.summary: Probability lies between 0 and 1.
lessons.example: 3 red out of 8 total gives P(red)=3/8.
lessons.practice: Find P(not blue) if 5 of 12 counters are blue.

lessons.title: Experimental and theoretical probability
lessons.objective: Compare observed frequency with expected probability.
lessons.summary: Experimental probability approaches theoretical probability over many trials.
lessons.example: 42 heads in 80 tosses gives experimental P(head)=42/80.
lessons.practice: Predict successes in 300 trials when probability is 0.4.

lessons.title: Bearings
lessons.objective: Measure clockwise from north using three digits.
lessons.summary: Bearings are written such as 045° or 270°.

lessons.title: Midpoints
lessons.objective: Average corresponding coordinates.
lessons.summary: Midpoint = ((x₁+x₂)/2,(y₁+y₂)/2).

lessons.title: Translations
lessons.objective: Add a vector to every vertex.
lessons.summary: A translation preserves shape and orientation.

lessons.title: Reflections
lessons.objective: Reflect using equal perpendicular distance from mirror line.
lessons.summary: Reflections reverse orientation.
lessons.example: Reflect (4,2) in the y-axis to get (-4,2).

lessons.title: Rotations
lessons.objective: Specify centre, angle and direction.
lessons.summary: Rotations preserve distance from the centre.
lessons.example: A 90° anticlockwise rotation about origin sends (x,y) to (-y,x).

lessons.title: Enlargements
lessons.objective: Use centre and scale factor.
lessons.summary: Lengths multiply by scale factor; angles stay equal.
lessons.example: Scale factor 2 doubles every distance from the centre.
lessons.practice: Enlarge a triangle by scale factor 1.5.

lessons.title: Conversions
lessons.objective: Convert metric units accurately.
lessons.summary: Use powers of 10 and square/cube conversion factors.

lessons.title: Parallelogram and trapezium area
lessons.objective: Apply correct area formulae.
lessons.summary: Parallelogram A=bh; trapezium A=½(a+b)h.
lessons.practice: Find area of a parallelogram with base 13 and height 8.

lessons.title: Triangles
lessons.objective: Use A=½bh.
lessons.summary: The perpendicular height is used.
lessons.practice: Find a triangle’s height if area=40 and base=10.

lessons.title: Prisms and pyramids
lessons.objective: Use volume = cross-sectional area × length for prisms.
lessons.summary: Keep units consistent.
lessons.example: A prism with cross-section 15 cm² and length 8 cm has volume 120 cm³.
lessons.practice: Find volume of a triangular prism with triangle area 24 cm² and length 10 cm.

lessons.title: Frequency diagrams
lessons.objective: Read and construct frequency diagrams.
lessons.summary: Frequency is shown by height or area depending on diagram type.
lessons.example: A bar of height 12 represents frequency 12.

lessons.title: Time series
lessons.objective: Identify trends and seasonal patterns.
lessons.summary: Time order is essential.
lessons.practice: Describe the trend in a monthly-sales graph.

lessons.title: Stem-and-leaf
lessons.objective: Organise data while retaining original values.
lessons.summary: Include a key and order leaves.

lessons.title: Pie charts
lessons.objective: Convert frequencies to angles.
lessons.summary: Sector angle = frequency/total × 360°.
lessons.practice: Find the angle for frequency 18 out of 72.

lessons.title: Statistics
lessons.objective: Compare centre and spread.
lessons.summary: Use mean/median/mode with range or other spread.
lessons.example: Same mean does not imply same consistency.
lessons.practice: Compare two groups using median and range.
